items.name: Handala (Iran-linked hacktivist group)
items.targets: Stryker medical technology company, Verifone payment systems
items.ttps: T1485 (Data Destruction/Wiper), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1082 (System Information Discovery)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Hundreds of Salesforce Experience Cloud sites, ongoing campaign targeting 400+ organizations
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.targets: AllSecure CEO via fake LinkedIn interview, developers via supply chain attacks
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain)

items.name: APT28 (Russia, Fancy Bear)
items.targets: Ukrainian military forces in long-term espionage operations, Signal and WhatsApp accounts
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1056 (Input Capture), T1005 (Data from Local System)

items.name: Chinese APT (Nexus Actors)
items.targets: Qatar government and organizations amid Middle East conflict, PlugX malware deployment
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol)

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery)

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: Everest Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.name: CLOP Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: Coinbase Cartel
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: BeatBanker Trojan operators
items.targets: Android users via phishing campaigns deploying banking Trojan and crypto miner
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1410 (Network Traffic Capture or Redirection), T1496 (Resource Hijacking)

items.name: DarkWebInformer (Threat Intelligence source)
items.targets: Viking Line Ferries passenger database, South Korean government server with lateral movement access, Maple Respiratory Colombia (1.8M patient records), Spanish Ministry of Finance employee data, US Clinic (150K patient records)
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1021 (Remote Services), T1534 (Internal Spearphishing)

items.name: GhostClaw/GhostLoader
items.targets: Developers via fake OpenClaw npm package masquerading as legitimate tools
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), T1059.007 (Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: A0Backdoor operators
items.targets: Organizations via Teams and Zoom social engineering campaigns
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Tycoon 2FA (Phishing-as-a-Service)
items.targets: Multiple organizations through phishing kit (platform disrupted by law enforcement)
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), T1528 (Steal Application Access Token)

canada_landscape.summary: In the past 48 hours, Canadian entities have been impacted by multiple threat campaigns. Canada Goose was reportedly breached by the Coinbase Cartel ransomware group. Meta disabled 150K accounts linked to Southeast Asia scam centers affecting Canadian users. Additionally, phishing campaigns using malicious ISO attachments in resumes and .arpa domain abuse for phishing evasion are actively targeting Canadian organizations and citizens.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose reported breach by Coinbase Cartel ransomware group
